doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’ve been having this, like, really bad pain in my right elbow for the past three days. It’s mostly on the outside, and it gets worse when I grip things or extend my wrist. Honestly, it’s been pretty uncomfortable.

doctor_turn: Can you point to where exactly the pain is located?
patient_turn: Yeah, it’s, um, right on the outer part of my elbow, like, over the lateral epicondyle. It really hurts there, especially when I try to grip something or extend my wrist.

doctor_turn: Does the pain ever spread to other areas, like your forearm or wrist?
patient_turn: Not really, um, it’s mostly just in the elbow area. I don’t feel any pain spreading to my forearm or wrist, just, like, sharp pain when I extend my wrist. It’s pretty localized, I guess.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your elbow?
patient_turn: Uh, I think it started a few days ago, but actually,I feel like it’s been going on for longer.

doctor_turn: How would you describe the pain in your elbow? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: Um, it’s kind of achy when I’m at rest, but then it gets really sharp when I extend my wrist. I’d say it’s about a 6 or 7 out of 10 in terms of severity, which is, like, pretty uncomfortable.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your elbow?
patient_turn: Oh, um, I noticed it about three days ago. It just kind of started out of nowhere and then got worse pretty quickly. I’m not really sure what caused it.

doctor_turn: Does the pain stay in your elbow, or does it spread to other areas?
patient_turn: Oh, you know, I really need to get my car serviced soon. Anyway, the pain is mostly in my elbow, but sometimes I think about how much I love pizza. It’s just so good!

doctor_turn: Does resting or taking ibuprofen make the pain feel any better?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, resting definitely helps a bit, and taking ibuprofen does make it feel better, too. It’s not completely gone, but it’s, like, more manageable when I do those things.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any numbness or tingling in your arm or hand?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t noticed any numbness or tingling, um, just the pain in my elbow. It’s mostly just that sharp pain when I move my wrist, but no other weird sensations, you know?

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any fever or unexpected weight loss lately?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any fever or, um, unexpected weight loss. I feel pretty normal aside from the elbow pain, honestly. Just, like, a bit worried about it.

doctor_turn: Have you had any headaches or feelings of dizziness recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any headaches or dizziness, um, just the elbow pain. Everything else feels pretty normal, but this pain is really bothering me.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any issues with your breathing or any heart-related symptoms?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t noticed any issues with my breathing or, um, any heart-related symptoms. It’s really just the pain in my elbow that’s been concerning me.

doctor_turn: Have you experienced any changes in your digestion or urination lately?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any changes in my digestion or urination, um, it’s really just the elbow pain that’s been on my mind. Everything else seems fine, I guess.

doctor_turn: Have you had any rashes or traveled anywhere recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any rashes or, um, traveled anywhere recently. It’s just this elbow pain that started a few days ago, and I’m kinda worried about it.

doctor_turn: Do you have any other medical conditions or past health issues I should know about?
patient_turn: Um, no, I don’t have any other medical conditions or past health issues. My health has been pretty good overall, aside from this elbow pain, which is, like, really frustrating.

doctor_turn: Are you currently taking any medications on a regular basis?
patient_turn: No, I’m not taking any medications regularly, um, just the occasional ibuprofen for the elbow pain. That’s about it, really.

doctor_turn: Are your immunizations up to date?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, my immunizations are up to date. I’ve kept up with everything, so that’s not an issue. It’s just this elbow pain that’s bothering me.

doctor_turn: Do you have any allergies to medications or anything else?
patient_turn: No, I don’t have any allergies, um, to medications or anything else. I’ve never had a problem with that, so that’s good, I guess.

doctor_turn: Does anyone in your family have joint or heart issues?
patient_turn: Well, my dad has had knee and hip pain, but, um, no one else in my family has any joint or heart issues that I know of. My aunt had uterine cancer, but that’s about it.

doctor_turn: Can you tell me about your living situation and who you live with?
patient_turn: Sure, um, I live with my wife and our four-year-old daughter. It’s a pretty busy household, you know? I just want to be able to play with them without this elbow pain getting in the way.

doctor_turn: How often do you drink alcohol, and how much do you usually have?
patient_turn: I, um, drink moderately, like, a glass of wine or a beer every few days. It’s not a lot, but I enjoy it, you know? I just hope it’s not affecting my elbow pain or anything.
